1N board 472: Play the ♣9 — third hand high, but from a solid run play the cheapest: it wins as well as the ♣J and shows partner the ♣J-10 above.
1M-3N_Picture_Bid board 307: Partner led low and dummy followed low, so it is up to you — play the ♣J, third hand high. Don't duck: your highest card makes declarer spend an honour to win.
1M-3N_Picture_Bid board 249: Play the ♦Q — your top card, third hand high: the queen wins, or drives out a higher honour. A small card hands declarer a free trick.
1M-3N_Picture_Bid board 62: Play the ♣K — third hand high: the king wins, or forces the ace. Play low and declarer scores a cheap trick.
1N board 32: From your ♠Q-J play the lower, the ♠J: equal cards win equally, so play the cheaper and keep the ♠Q working.
1M-3N_Picture_Bid board 16: Play the ♣10, the cheapest of your ♣Q-J-10: it wins as well as the ♣Q and tells partner you hold the ♣Q-J above it.
